narrative: Your loan application for 1108 Deutsche Marks to cover repairs was carefully reviewed by our credit model, which assesses risks based on patterns from past borrowers in 1970s Germany. The model calculated a 51.5% probability that you might face challenges repaying, tipping the decision toward denying the application to protect both you and the bank from potential difficulties. At the top of what influenced this, your checking account status—showing a balance under 0 DM—stood out as a major concern, as it often signals tighter cash flow compared to most approved applicants who had no such negative balances. Closely following that, your credit history of no prior credits or all paid back raised a red flag for the model; while it might seem positive, it's quite rare among those approved, who typically had a track record of existing paid credits, making yours appear riskier by comparison. On a brighter note, your real estate property pushed strongly in the opposite direction, helping toward a good credit outlook since owning such assets is a solid plus seen in many successful cases. Your relatively short requested duration of 12 months and low installment rate of 4% of disposable income were helpful too, but they couldn't outweigh the top concerns. Compared to typical approved applicants—who often sought larger amounts around 3200 DM with longer terms and stronger account histories—your profile aligned more with patterns the model links to higher repayment hurdles. With steady 4-7 years of employment as a skilled worker, owning your housing, and no other installment plans or debtors, you have many strengths that nearly balanced things out. Unfortunately, these key factors together nudged the prediction just over the line into bad credit risk territory. To improve your chances next time, consider building a more established credit history through smaller, successful loans, padding your checking account above zero, or providing extra details on your stable foreign worker background and real estate. We recommend waiting a few months, addressing the account status, and reapplying with any updates to your financial picture. This way, you can showcase the reliable side of your situation more clearly.
